gap_summaries.description: build_gate_file (verdict_engine.py) never stores breaking_hash in the gate file profile section. The breaking-hash reuse fallback reads gate_file.profile.breaking_hash, which would always be empty. Without enrichment in run_production_gate, the semver reuse path is dead code.
gap_summaries.fix: Added run_production_gate enrichment step: after evaluate_gate returns, inject breaking_hash via compute_breaking_hash and re-persist the gate file for semver profiles.

gap_summaries.description: Task 4 creates compute_gate_metrics with hardcoded flaky_categories=[] and timeout_categories=[]. Task 5 adds the detector functions but the prose instruction 'Update compute_gate_metrics to call these detectors' lacks code. An implementer could miss this, causing integration tests (Task 17) to fail because metrics.flaky_categories would always be empty.
gap_summaries.fix: Added explicit code showing the updated return dict calling detect_flaky_categories(history) and detect_timeout_categories(history). Added two integration-level tests for compute_gate_metrics verifying the fields are populated.

gap_summaries.description: After apply_calibrations returns, the profile version is never bumped. The GateCalibrationAudit event would report identical old_version and new_version. Profile snapshots would not reflect that calibrations were applied.
gap_summaries.fix: Added version bump after apply_calibrations: if any applied proposals have change_type='breaking', bump breaking; otherwise bump feature. Placed before the audit event emission so old_version/new_version reflect the actual change.

gap_summaries.description: Tests reference _make_valid_profile() helper function that does not exist in the test file. All six VersionDictValidationTests would fail with NameError.
gap_summaries.fix: Added _make_valid_profile() helper definition to the plan with all required profile fields, placed near the top of the test file before any test class.

gap_summaries.description: Breaking-hash enrichment requires a second persist_gate_file call after evaluate_gate, which already persists internally. This double-write is acceptable (gate evaluation is orders of magnitude more expensive) but creates a brief window where the on-disk file lacks breaking_hash.
gap_summaries.fix: Documented in the plan. Alternative (modifying verdict_engine.py) is blocked by the 'do not modify m1-m9' constraint.

gap_summaries.description: The fallback uses string matching ('profile.hash mismatch' in reason) against can_reuse_gate_file's error message. If that message format changes, the fallback silently stops working.
gap_summaries.fix: The exact string is verified against evidence_io.py line 216. The coupling is acceptable because both modules are under the same project's control and the string is a stable part of the interface.

gap_summaries.description: run_learning_loop returns updated_profile but does not persist it. The operator must manually save the calibrated profile. This is by design (operator controls profile changes) but could surprise an integrator.
gap_summaries.fix: Intentional: the learning loop proposes and applies in-memory; persistence is the caller's responsibility. The retrospective summary provides visibility into what changed.

gap_summaries.description: Duplicate _BASE_PROFILE fixtures in test_profile_versioning.py and test_profile_calibrator.py. These could diverge over time if profile schema changes.
gap_summaries.fix: Acceptable for test isolation. A shared test_fixtures.py module would add coupling between test files.

gap_summaries.description: compute_category_trends window test uses assertIn(trends.get('security'), ('improving', 'stable')) which accepts two outcomes.
gap_summaries.fix: The test correctly accepts both because with only 3 PASS results, the first/second half comparison depends on the 10% tolerance threshold.

gap_summaries.description: No CLI command for running the learning loop or viewing gate history.
gap_summaries.fix: CLI integration is a follow-up milestone. The learning loop is designed to be called programmatically from the orchestrator.

gap_summaries.description: No concurrent-write protection for history records beyond write_atomic. Timestamp-based filename collision is possible if two gate evaluations complete in the same second with the same gate_id.
gap_summaries.fix: Acceptable for single-user threat model (per project memory). The gate_id collision with same-second completion is extremely unlikely in practice.

gap_summaries.description: No CLI command to invoke pruning. prune_gate_history is available as Python API only.
gap_summaries.fix: CLI integration is a follow-up. Pruning could be wired into the learning loop itself as an optional step.
